title: Installers tolerate a present-but-broken runtime CLI in repo-copy mode; plugin-first fails with a named, actionable error
action: Mode-gate record: 1 flag (cross-platform), 3 product files -> small lane. Fix scripts/install.sh probe_plugin_state (line ~259 and the symmetric claude branch at ~264): when the runtime CLI is on PATH but `<cli> plugin list --json` exits nonzero, (a) DISTRIBUTION_MODE=plugin-first -> fail with a message that names the broken CLI and the ways out (repair the CLI, or re-run with --distribution repo-copy, or --runtime without that CLI); (b) repo-copy -> print a one-line warn via log() that the CLI was found but is not runnable and that repo-copy does not require it, rewrite the state json file to [] (the failed probe may have written partial output), and continue. Same treatment in scripts/install.ps1 (~line 214-217 codex, ~236-239 claude): repo-copy list-probe failure becomes Write-Warning + keep the pre-seeded '[]' state instead of Fail; plugin-first keeps Fail but with the named-CLI actionable message (note: ps1 plugin-first already fails earlier at marketplace add if the CLI is broken — keep the list-probe Fail as backstop). Add 2 e2e cases to scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs using the existing BEE_FAKE_FAIL mechanism (codex:list): (1) default repo-copy install with BEE_FAKE_FAIL=codex:list -> exit 0, output matches a warn about codex not runnable, target onboarded; (2) plugin-first install with BEE_FAKE_FAIL=codex:list -> nonzero exit, output names codex as present-but-broken and mentions repo-copy as a way out. Follow the existing check() style of the file.

truths: a broken codex on PATH no longer blocks a default (repo-copy) install; the install completes with a one-line warning
plugin-first with a broken required CLI still fails, and the message names the CLI and at least one concrete way out
rollback_plugin's re-probe path degrades the same way (no fail inside rollback for repo-copy)
no existing e2e assertion changes; the two new cases pass alongside the full suite
prohibitions: do not silence the CLI's own stderr diagnostics
do not make plugin-first tolerant — a working CLI is a real requirement there
do not restructure the probe/transition/rollback flow beyond the failure handling

trace.outcome: Both installers tolerate a present-but-broken runtime CLI in repo-copy (one-line warn, empty probe state, install proceeds); plugin-first still refuses with a named CLI and concrete fix options; 2 new e2e cases green
trace.verify_output: test_installers_e2e (bash): 21 passed, 0 failed — incl. new cases 16 (broken codex on PATH: default repo-copy install succeeds with a warning; target onboarded) and 16a (broken codex on PATH: plugin-first still fails, names codex and repo-copy as the way out, zero mutations). Orchestrator's own fresh run, exit 0.
